title: Bidens cronquistii
intro: Bidens cronquistii, also known as Cronquist's beggarticks, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Bidens cronquistii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Bidens cronquistii is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, flu, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Bidens cronquistii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Mexico and has been identified as part of the Bidens genus, which comprises around 150 species of plants. The Bidens genus is further grouped into the tribe Helenieae, a division of the subfamily Asteroideae. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other members of the Asteraceae family. Through its classification, Bidens cronquistii is recognized as a unique and distinct species within the broader context of plant taxonomy.
